caption:
wait until you see the bottom floors 

#japan #travel #reels #instagramreels #igreels
transcript: What can you actually do at 2 o'clock in the morning in Japan? A while ago, I talked about that building you accidentally walked past in Japan that was filled with the wildest 11 stories of pure insanity. The best building in Japan. Well, today we're going back, and it's still only $10. Everything I show you is included and unlimited. Oh, but this time, we're going in the middle of the night. At 2 a.m., you can go batting in the rooftop batting cages. I did not do well. No time limit, by the way. Go-karting, dirt biking, like actual put-together races. Not skilled enough for that one. There's a roller rink like the 90s with free skate rentals, a full-blown sky zone trampoline area, and human soccer. Yes, you heard that right. The sports floor has basketball, got it, tennis, darts, which are a big deal in Japan, archery, robot ping-pong, but the next floor. A playground, a huge all-you-can-play arcade with rhythm games, a manga cafe and library, karaoke rooms. It is 2 a.m. There's mini bowling, a full bowling alley, two floors up, bull riding, pool, and a retro diner. This is round one Namba, just off Don't Don't Borry. And if you go afternoon or late night on weekdays, the price sometimes gets as low as $10 for all of it. And I didn't even talk about what's in the basement. Next video. Next video. Next video.
